acceptance.baseline: R1 requires auditing our decode path against MHR conversion.py@4debaacf L472-516: pred_vertices and pred_cam_t both get the same 100x cm/m scale at the same point; axis flip is branch-dependent.
acceptance.after: PASS audit complete. Local conversion.py is not vendored, so the binding reference used is runs/research_w6refresh_20260709/sota_body_synthesis.md:5-15 plus local read-only Fast-SAM reference third_party/Fast-SAM-3D-Body/mhr2smpl/multi_view/step1a_collect_fitted.py:134-138. Our side verdicts: scripts/racketsport/run_sam3dbody_batch.py:2091-2108 captures pred_keypoints_3d, pred_vertices, pred_cam_t, scale_params, pred_joint_coords correctly; threed/racketsport/hmr_deep.py:651-705 prefers pred_keypoints_3d over pred_joint_coords, preserves camera_translation from pred_cam_t, but passes joints_camera/vertices_camera without applying it (incorrect/incomplete vs conversion); threed/racketsport/mhr_decode.py:345-364 passes MHR scale_params into mhr_forward (correct for latent decode, not the conversion 100x); threed/racketsport/mhr_decode.py:400-407 flips Y/Z unconditionally in decoded latent output (ambiguous vs branch-dependent reference); threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2556-2605 grounds camera offsets by track_world_xy/floor and does not read camera_translation (our extrapolation, not conversion.py).
acceptance.target: Report file:line for every scale/axis application on our side, with correct/incorrect/ambiguous verdict.

acceptance.baseline: R1 says conversion.py never reads joint fields, so there is no maintainer-blessed world-skeleton formula.
acceptance.after: PASS. Our formula is threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2556-2605: parse joints_camera/vertices_camera, rotate camera offsets to world with calibration R at threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2566-2577, choose a low-grounding anchor at threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2578-2579, translate by dx=track_world_xy[0]-anchor_xy[0], dy=track_world_xy[1]-anchor_xy[1], dz=-min_z at threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2582-2584, then emit translated joints_world/vertices_world at threed/racketsport/worldhmr.py:2603-2605. Assumptions: track_world_xy is trusted, court floor is z=0, low body points anchor the feet, and camera_motion is absent in the decode helper.
acceptance.target: Document the exact world-skeleton placement formula and assumptions as our extrapolation.

changes: threed/racketsport/mhr_decode.py:505 added optional pred_cam_t and pred_cam_t_already_applied to ground_decoded_camera_frame so harness-side camera translation policy is explicit.
threed/racketsport/mhr_decode.py:550 added apply_pred_cam_t_once for exactly-once translation of joints and vertices.
scripts/racketsport/synthetic_body_decode_gate.py:1 added CPU-runnable synthetic BODY decode-fidelity gate CLI with mock decoder and VM-facing sam3d adapter shape.
tests/racketsport/test_mhr_decode.py:66 added a unit test that fails if pred_cam_t is missing or double-applied in ground_decoded_camera_frame.
tests/racketsport/test_synthetic_body_decode_gate.py:12 added direct CLI, output-metric, render-file, help, and scaffold-index tests for the new synthetic gate.
runs/lanes/w7_p22checklist_20260709/synthetic_body_decode_gate_report.json:1 generated CPU mock synthetic gate evidence.
honest_issues: Objective is BLOCKED, not PASS: pred_cam_t exactly-once is not proven end-to-end in the production body normalizer/grounding path because the required wiring lives outside this lane's owned files.

Proposed fenced diff inline: in threed/racketsport/hmr_deep.py normalize_fast_sam_body_output, read camera_translation once from pred_cam_t, pass joints and vertices through mhr_decode.apply_pred_cam_t_once(..., pred_cam_t=camera_translation) before assigning joints_camera/vertices_camera, and preserve camera_translation for traceability; if a future raw source proves sidecars are already translated, add an explicit pred_cam_t_already_applied flag instead of silently relying on convention. Then rerun GATE-1b on a raw body_mesh with pred_cam_t present.

Alternate skeleton-field quantification is blocked by missing local W6 raw sidecars/body_mesh.json under runs/lanes/w6_close_errand_20260708/gpu_instrument_wolverine_mixed_0200_raw_postchain; only postchain skeleton/body mesh index evidence is present locally.
The ceiling-rule workaround is not recommended yet: 53.5mm p95 looks family-normal, but the 262.35mm outlier remains unadjudicated.
No best_stack promotion, no gate threshold change, no eval protocol change, no process_video change, and no protected label edits were made.

The git worktree remains dirty from other lanes, including protected/cross-lane files such as configs/racketsport/best_stack.json, scripts/racketsport/process_video.py, tests/racketsport/test_best_stack_manifest.py, tests/racketsport/test_process_video.py, and unrelated untracked labelpacks; I did not revert or clean them.
next: Manager/owner decision needed for the fenced pred_cam_t production wiring in threed/racketsport/hmr_deep.py/worldhmr path, followed by a rerun that preserves raw pred_cam_t, pred_vertices, pred_keypoints_3d, and pred_joint_coords sidecars so GATE-1b and alternate-field divergence can be recomputed. Only after that should BEST-STACK get a PENDING decode-fidelity entry tied to synthetic_body_decode_gate plus real GATE-1b evidence.
